note: DEMO (#1589): a single shared-rule edit re-proved across two affected grid-puzzle levels. The directly-edited level (rule-edited-target) stays design-clean, but the SAME rule edit opens a brand-new unintended over-solution in another level ELSEWHERE (shared-mechanic-elsewhere) — the crate now sits one push from its target, so the level can be won in a single move while the designer intended a longer scenic path. The harness re-runs the existing solver + over-solution detector + difficulty suite, diffs against the recorded baseline, and flags the elsewhere level as a regression with a replayable counterexample trace (["left"] — replay it on the runtime to watch the bypass). Detection only; no auto-fix, no apply, no promotion.
guardrails: Re-runs the existing #1580 solver, #1581 over-solution detector, and #1582 difficulty suite; no new comparison engine.
Every flagged regression carries a replayable trace.
Detection only: no apply, auto-apply, promote, merge, or self-approve; trusted writes stay on the existing review/apply/trust-gradient path.
Deterministic and fixture-scoped; no network or live browser.
Generated re-run artifacts stay untracked under generatedOutputRoot.
